preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar click [ǃ] to the voiceless velar stop [k] is a classic example of 'click loss'. Because clicks require a primary anterior closure (at the alveolar ridge) and a secondary posterior closure (at the velum), the loss of the complex suction mechanism often results in speakers retaining only the rear velar articulation.

examples.note: When Tswana borrowed click words from Nguni languages, it systematically replaced the voiceless alveolar clicks with voiceless velar stops.
In these East Kalahari Khoe languages, the alveolar click frequently shifts to a velar stop or affricate.
